message:
feat(copilot): add Copilot CLI event log (JSONL) parsing and session-state discovery

Add comprehensive support for GitHub Copilot CLI session history, which
stores conversations in a different format than the VS Code Copilot Chat
extension. The CLI uses JSONL event logs (one JSON object per line) in
~/.copilot/session-state/{session-id}/events.jsonl (v2, since 0.0.342)
and legacy single-JSON files in ~/.copilot/history-session-state/ (v1).

Connector changes (src/connectors/copilot.rs):

- Expand module-level documentation with a new "Copilot CLI event logs"
  section describing the JSONL format, storage paths, and event types
  (user.message, assistant.message with content/role/timestamp fields).

- Add std::io::BufRead import for line-by-line JSONL parsing.

- Extend gh_copilot_paths() to include ~/.copilot/session-state and
  ~/.copilot/history-session-state alongside existing gh-copilot paths.

- Update looks_like_copilot_storage() with a new windows(2) check that
  recognizes ".copilot" + "session-state" or "history-session-state"
  path segment pairs as valid Copilot storage locations.

- Broaden find_conversation_files() to discover .jsonl files in addition
  to .json, both when given a single file path and when walking
  directories (max_depth 4).

- Add is_cli_event_log() to detect CLI event log files by extension
  (.jsonl), filename (events.jsonl), or parent directory name
  (session-state / history-session-state).

- Add parse_cli_event_log() to parse JSONL event logs line-by-line,
  extracting session_id, workspace/cwd, and time bounds from events,
  then assembling NormalizedMessage entries for user/assistant events.
  Gracefully skips malformed lines and non-message event types.

- Add parse_cli_session_json() to handle legacy v1 session-state JSON
  files that use an "events" or "history" array within a single JSON
  document, falling back to the existing VS Code parser for files with
  "turns", "messages", or "conversations" keys.

- Add parse_conversation_file_from_value() helper to route a parsed
  serde_json::Value through the existing conversation parser, avoiding
  redundant file I/O when content is already deserialized.

- Add extract_cli_event_message() to determine role and content from CLI
  events, supporting multiple naming conventions: user.message /
  assistant.message (documented), userPromptSubmitted / assistantResponse
  (hook events), prompt / response / completion (generic), and explicit
  "role" fields. Content extraction falls back through "prompt",
  "initialPrompt", "output", and "result" fields when standard
  extraction yields empty text.

- Update the scan() dispatcher to route files identified as CLI event
  logs to parse_cli_event_log() instead of parse_conversation_file().

- Add scan_roots candidate paths for .copilot/session-state and
  .copilot/history-session-state within each scan root.

- Add 11 new tests covering: basic JSONL parsing with session metadata,
  hook-style event type names, legacy JSON session files, prompt/output
  field extraction, non-message event filtering, empty event files,
  scan_roots integration, is_cli_event_log detection, path recognition,
  multiple concurrent sessions, malformed line resilience, and metadata
  source tagging ("copilot-cli").

Probe path changes (src/lib.rs):

- Add ~/.copilot/session-state and ~/.copilot/history-session-state to
  the "github-copilot" slug in default_probe_roots().

- Add the same two paths (with tilde notation) to
  default_probe_paths_tilde() for SSH-based remote scanning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
